docs.title: freshrss-agent
docs.text:
FreshRSS API + MCP Server + A2A Agent for the agent-utilities ecosystem — a typed, action-routed connector.

Official documentation

This site is the canonical reference for freshrss-agent, maintained alongside every release.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
freshrss-agent wraps the target service with typed, deterministic MCP tools and an optional Pydantic-AI agent server.

The connector remains inactive when credentials are absent: configure FRESHRSS_URL and FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD to connect it to an instance.

docs.title: Explore the documentation
docs.text:
• Installation — pip, source, extras, and the prebuilt Docker image.
  • Deployment — run the MCP and agent servers, Docker Compose, Caddy + Technitium.
  • Usage — the MCP tools, the Python client, and the CLI.
  • Backing Platform — deploy the target service with Docker.
  • Overview — the action-routed tool surface and architecture.
  • Concepts — the CONCEPT ID registry.

docs.text: Prefix: CONCEPT:FRSS-* Version: 0.1.0 Bridge: CONCEPT:ECO-4.0 (Unified Toolkit Ingestion)

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
This page covers running freshrss-agent as long-lived servers.

freshrss-agent ships both an MCP server (console script freshrss-mcp) and an A2A agent server (console script freshrss-agent).

docs.title: Deployment Options
docs.text:
freshrss-agent exposes its MCP server (console script freshrss-mcp) four ways. Pick the row that matches where the server runs relative to your MCP client, then copy the matching mcp_config.json below.

# Option Transport Where it runs mcp_config.json key 1 stdio stdio client launches a subprocess command 2 Streamable-HTTP (local) streamable-http a local network port command or url 3 Local container / uv stdio or streamable-http Docker / Podman / uv on this host command or url 4 Remote URL streamable-http a remote host behind Caddy url

docs.title: 1. stdio (local subprocess)
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "freshrss-mcp": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["--from", "freshrss-agent", "freshrss-mcp"],
      "env": {
        "FRESHRSS_URL": "https://service.example.com",
        "FRESHRSS_USER": "admin",
        "FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD": "your_api_password"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 2. Streamable-HTTP (local process)
docs.text:
uvx --from freshrss-agent freshrss-mcp --transport streamable-http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health        # {"status":"OK"}

Connect to the running process by URL:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "freshrss-mcp": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp" }
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Local container / uv
docs.text:
Launch a container directly from mcp_config.json (swap docker for podman for a daemonless runtime):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "freshrss-mcp": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": [
        "run", "-i", "--rm",
        "-e", "TRANSPORT=stdio",
        "-e", "FRESHRSS_URL=https://service.example.com",
        "-e", "FRESHRSS_USER=admin",
        "-e", "FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD=your_api_password",
        "knucklessg1/freshrss-agent:latest"
      ]
    }
  }
}

Or run a local streamable-http container and connect by URL:

docker compose -f docker/mcp.compose.yml up -d

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "freshrss-mcp": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp" }
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Remote URL (deployed behind Caddy)
docs.text:
When the server is deployed remotely and published through Caddy on the internal *.arpa zone, connect with the "url" key — no local process or image required:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "freshrss-mcp": { "url": "http://freshrss-mcp.arpa/mcp" }
  }
}

Caddy reverse-proxies http://freshrss-mcp.arpa to the container's :8000 streamable-http listener.

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
docker compose -f docker/mcp.compose.yml up -d      # MCP server only
docker compose -f docker/agent.compose.yml up -d    # MCP + agent

docs.title: Run the A2A agent server
docs.text: freshrss-agent --mcp-config mcp_config.json --web

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: freshrss-agent is a standard Python package and a prebuilt container image.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 – 3.14.
  • A reachable target service instance and access token.

docs.title: From PyPI (recommended)
docs.text: pip install freshrss-agent

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text: Extra Install Pulls in mcp pip install "freshrss-agent[mcp]" FastMCP MCP-server runtime (agent-utilities[mcp]) agent pip install "freshrss-agent[agent]" Pydantic-AI agent + Logfire tracing all pip install "freshrss-agent[all]" Everything above

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Knuckles-Team/freshrss-agent.git
cd freshrss-agent
pip install -e ".[all]"

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: docker pull knucklessg1/freshrss-agent:latest

docs.title: freshrss-agent — Concept Overview
docs.text: Category: Integration | Ecosystem Role: MCP Server + A2A Agent Built on agent-utilities — the unified AGI Harness.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: freshrss-agent wraps a self-hosted FreshRSS RSS reader through its Google Reader compatible API (GReader, served at {FRESHRSS_URL}/api/greader.php). It exposes two action-routed MCP tool domains — freshrss_reader (stream contents, item bodies, unread counts) and freshrss_subscriptions (subscribe/unsubscribe/label feeds, browse categories, mark items read or starred) — plus a Python API and an A2A agent. The reader's stream_contents action is consumed directly by the agent-utilities Knowledge Graph connector preset to ingest feed items.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
This project follows the standardized agent-package pattern:

  • Modular Design: split into api/ (client mixins) and mcp/ (action-routed tool modules) for cleaner organization.
  • Dynamic Tool Registration: action-routed dynamic tool tags, strictly lowercase, each togglable with a *TOOL environment flag.
  • A2A Agent Server: a Pydantic-AI graph agent (console script freshrss-agent) that calls the MCP tool surface and exposes an AG-UI web interface.

docs.title: Concept Registry
docs.text:
This project implements or inherits the following ecosystem concepts:

Concept ID Description Source ECO-4.1 MCP & Universal Skills agent-utilities (inherited) ECO-4.2 A2A Network & Consensus agent-utilities (inherited)

📖 Full Registry: See agent-utilities/docs/overview.md for the complete 5-Pillar concept index.

docs.title: Backing Platform — FreshRSS
docs.text:
freshrss-agent is a client of a backing service instance. This page provides a Docker recipe for deploying one locally to serve as the target of FRESHRSS_URL.

Backing-system recipe

Each connector in the ecosystem follows the same convention — a docs/platform.md recipe for the system it integrates with, accompanied by a sample Compose stack. Systems offered only as a managed service have no local recipe.

docs.title: Single-node deployment (Compose)
docs.text:
# docker/platform.compose.yml — FreshRSS backing instance
services:
  freshrss:
    image: freshrss/freshrss:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: Etc/UTC
      CRON_MIN: "*/15"
    volumes:
      - freshrss_data:/var/www/FreshRSS/data
      - freshrss_extensions:/var/www/FreshRSS/extensions
    ports:
      - "8080:80"

volumes:
  freshrss_data:
  freshrss_extensions:

After the container is up, complete the web installer at http://localhost:8080, then enable the API under Settings → Authentication → Allow API access and set an API password for your user. freshrss-agent authenticates against the GReader endpoint using:

  • FRESHRSS_URL — base URL of the instance (e.g. http://freshrss.arpa).
  • FRESHRSS_USER — the FreshRSS username (the GReader Email field).
  • FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD — the API password set in FreshRSS (not the login password).

docs.title: Usage — API / CLI / MCP
docs.text: freshrss-agent exposes the same capability three ways: as MCP tools an agent calls, as a Python API you import, and as a CLI.

docs.title: As an MCP server
docs.text:
Once deployed, the server registers consolidated, action-routed tool modules. Each module is independently togglable with a *TOOL environment flag.

The MCP surface is split into two action-routed tools:

  • freshrss_reader (toggle READERTOOL) — actions stream_contents, item_contents, unread_count. stream_contents returns the raw {"items": [...], "continuation": "..."} payload with full item bodies, and accepts stream_id, count, order, newer_than (a unix-seconds delta watermark) and continuation.
  • freshrss_subscriptions (toggle SUBSCRIPTIONSTOOL) — actions list, subscribe, unsubscribe, label, categories, mark_read, star.

docs.title: As a Python API
docs.text:
from freshrss_agent.auth import get_client

# reads FRESHRSS_URL / FRESHRSS_USER / FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD from the environment / .env
api = get_client()
page = api.stream_contents(count=50)          # {"items": [...], "continuation": "..."}
subs = api.subscription_list()
api.mark_read([item["id"] for item in page["items"]])

docs.title: As a CLI
docs.text:
export FRESHRSS_URL="http://freshrss.arpa"
export FRESHRSS_USER="admin"
export FRESHRSS_API_PASSWORD="your_api_password"
freshrss-mcp --transport stdio
